title: new MongoClient with tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true — the database TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: MongoClient validates the server certificate (tlsAllowInvalidCertificates is not true) — the database connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new MongoClient is created with tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true, which turns OFF validation of the MongoDB server's TLS certificate. Anyone on the network path can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the database connection — every query, credential, and record is exposed. Remove tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true; provide the proper CA via tlsCAFile if a private CA is used.
params.absentCallDetail: No MongoClient construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: MongoClient does not set tlsAllowInvalidCertificates; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: The native mongodb driver twin of the mongoose rule. tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true is pasted to silence a self-signed-cert error against a managed cluster and ships, disabling MITM protection on the database connection.
